answer:
Oh, that's a great question! I would say one of the best examples is cooking. You know, at first, it can feel pretty daunting—especially when you’re trying to follow a recipe step by step. But really, the magic happens when you get in the kitchen and just start experimenting. I mean, sure, you might burn a few things or over-salt your soup once or twice, but that’s all part of the learning curve, right?

The more you cook, the more you get a feel for flavors and techniques. It’s like building muscle memory; you learn how to chop veggies quickly or how to tell when a steak is perfectly cooked just by looking at it. And then there’s the whole improvisation thing. Like, you might not have a specific ingredient, but you learn to substitute it with something else, which can lead to some pretty amazing dishes.

Not to mention, cooking becomes more enjoyable over time. You start to feel like a bit of a pro in your own kitchen. So yeah, cooking is definitely one of those things where practice really makes perfect!
title: What's something you can only learn by doing it many times?
